cross_scene_arcs.title: Quest for Meaning in the Face of Oblivion
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Hazel moves from philosophical acceptance of oblivion to pursuing a concrete desire for answers from Peter Van Houten, facing financial and medical barriers, ultimately finding meaning not in the answers but in the shared journey with Gus.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: rejects legacy, embraces inevitability of being forgotten
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: accepts that meaning comes from shared experience, not external validation
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: lingering need for narrative closure about life and art

cross_scene_arcs.title: Development of Romantic and Emotional Intimacy with Gus
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Hazel's connection with Gus evolves from intellectual recognition to mutual affection, deepened by the Amsterdam trip and his selfless gesture, culminating in her confronting his mortality and publicly honoring their relationship at his funeral.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: initial mutual recognition through shared realism about death
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: publicly acknowledges and mourns Gus as her romantic partner
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: emotional aftermath of losing a first love and how to carry the relationship forward in memory

cross_scene_arcs.title: Struggle for Autonomy and Agency in Illness
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Hazel asserts her desire to live fully despite her terminal diagnosis, challenging medical authority and parental hesitation, gradually gaining agency in decision-making and self-expression.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: denied permission to travel due to medical risk
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: actively participates in planning and experiencing life on her own terms
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: tension between physical limitations and desire for independence

cross_scene_arcs.title: Mother-Daughter Relationship Amid Terminal Illness
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Hazel and her mother navigate shared guilt, financial strain, and emotional dependency, moving from unspoken fear to honest confrontation and mutual reassurance about their enduring bond beyond death.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: mutual guilt over unaffordable dream and emotional burden
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: open acknowledgment of love and continuity of maternal identity
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: fear of being forgotten or replaced after death

cross_scene_arcs.title: Confronting Mortality and Legacy
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Hazel progresses from detached skepticism about death talk to personally engaging with mortality through Gus's decline, ultimately accepting her role in mourning and memorializing him.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: emotionally detached from others' narratives about death
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: actively participates in rituals of remembrance and articulates the purpose of grief
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: how to reconcile personal grief with the absence of lasting legacy
